notes: Single Gate completion must not be treated as global completion.
P0/P1/P2 remain incomplete while remaining_gates is non-empty.
Do not enter P1 implementation before P0 backfill gates and rerun P0 Core Lifecycle Acceptance Gate.
GBrain supplement merged: P0-9 reserves memory/evidence metadata only; P1/P2 entries remain queued.
Capability Inventory routed P0 gaps into P0-4B OKF Minimal Core, P0-4C Agent Memory Minimal Core, and P0-5B Knowledge Reliability Minimal Core.
P0-4B OKF Minimal Core passed blackbox evidence with blocked rows 0 and now needs Owner Review.
Capability chain remaining_gates now follows the Capability Implementation Status V1 P0/P1/P2 order.
External project governance now classifies projects as real_integration, absorb, learn, reference, or reject before routing them to capability gates.
TencentDB Agent Memory is classified as absorb for P0-4C/P1/P2 planning; it is not integrated and must not add Node 22 dependencies in P0.
Dual-track acceptance is now required: Core Acceptance and Blackbox Acceptance are separate statuses.
Core passed plus Blackbox blocked must remain release blocked and cannot close P0.
Acceptance type now decides whether Blackbox, Core, Artifact, Event, Governance or Composite evidence is mandatory.
Composite capabilities do not need standalone UI blackbox, but must keep linked blackbox cases in the product paths they support.
OKF, Knowledge Reliability and Agent Memory are composite capabilities for P0 routing.
P0-4C Agent Memory Minimal Core has capability-level closure evidence and remains subject to P0 Release Gate.
P0-5B Knowledge Reliability Minimal Core has capability-level closure evidence and remains subject to P0 Core rerun plus P0 Release Gate.
P0 Core Lifecycle Acceptance backfill rerun has aggregated P0-1 through P0-10 plus P0-4B/P0-4C/P0-5B with blocked rows 0.
P0 queue now continues with P0 Release Gate before P1 entry.
Stage chain is P0 -> P0 Release Gate -> P1 -> P1 Release Gate -> P2 -> P2 Release Gate -> Final Owner Review.
Release Gate means staged exit review, not production release or public release claim.
P1 Release Gate regresses P0; P2 Release Gate regresses P0 plus P1 plus P2.

acceptance_model.core_acceptance: whitebox_or_greybox evidence that the underlying ability exists
acceptance_model.blackbox_acceptance: required for user_blackbox capabilities; composite capabilities use linked_blackbox_cases instead of standalone UI blackbox
acceptance_model.artifact_acceptance: required for durable outputs such as reports, documents, skills, exports and task memory snapshots
acceptance_model.event_acceptance: required when events are part of the capability contract, such as memory_snapshot_created
acceptance_model.governance_acceptance: required for registries, queues, status vocabularies and forbidden-claim boundaries
acceptance_model.composite_acceptance: OKF, Knowledge Reliability and Agent Memory need Core plus Artifact/Event/Governance evidence and linked product-path cases
